title: Spec Artifacts Configuration
description: Declares spec-schema pairings and behavior/conformance essentials for LLM artifact generation.

behaviorEssentials: Resolve presentation using deterministic cascade precedence: defaults, then matching selectors in document order, then item-specific overrides.
Apply token references in style and widget configuration at theme-application time; unresolved tokens should fall back to renderer defaults with diagnostics.
Treat platform as an open string hint; unknown platform values should not block theme application.
When preferred widgets are unavailable, apply declared fallback chains before reverting to renderer-native defaults.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming theme document must include $formspecTheme=1.0, version, and targetDefinition.
Renderers must not let theme content alter core data semantics, validation semantics, or response payload shape.
Implementations should validate targetDefinition compatibility ranges and warn on mismatch rather than hard-failing rendering.

behaviorEssentials: Execute rules in deterministic order after defaults and direction resolution; higher priority executes first and same-priority rules preserve document order.
Evaluate condition guards before transform application; skipped rules must not write target values.
Support explicit reverse overrides and bidirectional flags to control reverse execution behavior.
Treat adapters as serialization boundaries: mapping evaluates against structured data before format-specific output encoding.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming mapping document must include version, definitionRef, definitionVersion, targetSchema, and at least one rule.
Processors must reject invalid mapping documents and unknown transform declarations rather than silently ignoring them.
Reverse execution through non-reversible transforms must be rejected or explicitly disabled by rule configuration.

behaviorEssentials: Response documents are bound to an exact definition identity tuple and must be interpreted against that pinned version.
Response status transitions are business-process concerns, but completed responses must satisfy error-level validation expectations.
Validation findings can be carried inline through validationResults without changing the canonical response data payload.
Optional metadata fields (author, subject, id) should not alter structural validity of required response content.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming response must include definitionUrl, definitionVersion, status, data, and authored.
Processors must reject responses that do not conform to the response schema shape.
Pinned definition references must not be silently substituted with different versions during processing.

behaviorEssentials: Treat an Intake Handoff as the response-to-workflow boundary object, not as a Formspec-owned case lifecycle event.
Require workflowInitiated handoffs to include an existing caseRef and require publicIntake handoffs to omit or null caseRef.

For workflowInitiated attach acceptance, compare the handoff caseRef string to the accepted attach disposition (or host-defined equivalence); do not require only a canonical case id that never appeared on the handoff.
Preserve the pinned definitionRef tuple when accepting a handoff; do not substitute a newer Definition version.
Verify responseHash against the canonical Response envelope before using the handoff to create or update governed case state.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming Intake Handoff document must include $formspecIntakeHandoff=1.0 and the required response, validation, ledger, definition, mode, and timestamp fields.
Processors must reject publicIntake handoffs that carry a non-null caseRef and workflowInitiated handoffs without a caseRef.
Workflow or case hosts own governed case identity and case-created semantics; Formspec processors only produce intake evidence.
Hosts must not rewrite the emitted handoff JSON to substitute a canonical governed-case id for workflowInitiated caseRef.

behaviorEssentials: ValidationReport aggregates all findings at a point in time and should be treated as an immutable evaluation snapshot.
The valid flag must be derived from error-level findings, not from warning or info counts.
Counts must remain consistent with results so report summaries and downstream decisions stay deterministic.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming validation report must include valid, results, counts, and timestamp.
ValidationReport results entries must conform to the shared validation-result.schema.json schema.
Processors should reject reports where required severity counts are missing or invalid.

behaviorEssentials: Definition documents declare structure, logic hooks, and extension points but do not execute behavior by themselves.
Top-level lifecycle fields (status, version, versionAlgorithm) determine publication and compatibility semantics across versions.
Definition-level defaults and options (for example formPresentation and nonRelevantBehavior) establish baseline processing and rendering behavior.
Behavioral logic remains declarative through binds and shapes and is evaluated by conformant processors at runtime.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming definition must include $formspec, url, version, status, title, and items.
Processors must reject definitions that violate structural constraints or unsupported $formspec versions.
The (url, version) pair must be treated as immutable identity for definition pinning and response validation.

behaviorEssentials: Registry documents describe extension metadata and compatibility, but do not prescribe centralized hosting or governance.
Entry lifecycle states should guide consumer behavior for adoption, warning surfaces, and retirement handling.
Naming conventions and uniqueness constraints prevent collision and support cross-organization interoperability.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming registry document must include $formspecRegistry, publisher, published, and entries.
Registry entry names must follow x-prefixed naming rules and category-specific requirements.
Processors should reject malformed registries and conflicting duplicate name/version entry pairs.

behaviorEssentials: Changelog entries represent atomic diffs between two definition versions and should be processed in document order.
Impact classification should be derived deterministically from change semantics to drive semver governance.
Migration hints are advisory mappings that assist generation of formal migration artifacts.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming changelog document must include definitionUrl, fromVersion, toVersion, semverImpact, and changes.
Change objects must include type, target, path, and impact, with optional before/after details as applicable.
semverImpact must reflect the maximum impact level represented in the change set.

behaviorEssentials: Component documents define a single-root presentation tree where child order is rendered deterministically.
Slot binding links input components to definition item keys while preserving core behavioral semantics from the definition.
Tier 3 components take precedence over theme and inline hints for layout/widget selection, with fallback rendering for unbound items.
Core processors must substitute progressive components with declared fallbacks while preserving structure and applicable base props.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming component document must include $formspecComponent, version, targetDefinition, and tree.
Processors must reject unsupported component-document versions and unknown non-x top-level properties.
Renderable trees must satisfy component-category constraints, bind compatibility, and declared structural limits.

behaviorEssentials: Locale Documents are display-only sidecars — they MUST NOT affect data collection, validation logic, or behavioral semantics.
Resolve localized strings using a deterministic cascade: regional locale → base language locale → inline Definition defaults.
String keys use dot-delimited paths (itemKey.property) to target specific localizable properties; unmatched keys are silently ignored.
FEL interpolation via {{expression}} syntax is evaluated at render time against current form state; broken expressions fall back to the raw template string with a diagnostic.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming locale document must include $formspecLocale=1.0, version, locale, targetDefinition, and strings.
Processors MUST perform case-insensitive BCP 47 tag comparison and SHOULD normalize to lowercase language with title-case region.
Processors MUST detect circular fallback chains and terminate the cascade with a warning rather than looping.

behaviorEssentials: References Documents are standalone sidecars that bind external resources to Definition items by path — they MUST NOT alter data capture, validation, or the processing model.
Each Bound Reference targets a specific item path (or '#' for form-level); references do not inherit from parent groups to children.
The type and rel properties are open strings for forward compatibility — processors SHOULD warn on unrecognized values but MUST NOT reject the document.
Multiple References Documents may target the same Definition; their references are merged additively with document-internal order preserved.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming references document must include $formspecReferences=1.0, version, targetDefinition, and references.
Extended processors must verify targetDefinition.url matches the loaded Definition and resolve all $ref pointers at load time.
Extended processors must validate referenceDefs id-key matching and should warn on target paths that don't correspond to definition items.

behaviorEssentials: Kernel documents define the immutable topology (states and transitions) and case state of a workflow orchestration substrate.
The case file structure SHOULD mirror the data-capture definitions used for input in the workflow.
Transition logic must be deterministic; choice states MUST have exhaustive, mutually exclusive conditions.
Impact level classification established in the kernel determines baseline governance constraints for the entire workflow lifecycle.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming author-time workflow document must include `$wosWorkflow: "1.0"` plus required core fields `url`, `version`, `title`, `impactLevel`, `actors`, and `lifecycle` (merged envelope per ADR 0076).
Processors must reject workflow documents that do not conform to the workflow schema shape.
The (url, version) pair must be treated as immutable identity for pinning and execution.

behaviorEssentials: Workflow Governance sidecars attach to kernel workflows via semantic tags (lifecycleHook) and data contracts (contractHook).
Due process requirements (notice, explanation, appeal) are normative for rights-impacting or safety-impacting impact levels.
Review protocols like independentFirst must be enforced by the processor and UI to ensure cognitive engagement.
Data validation pipelines with assertion gates validate untrusted external data before it enters the workflow case state.
conformanceEssentials: Due-process and pipeline governance are expressed under the optional `governance` property inside an author-time document marked `$wosWorkflow: "1.0"` and validated by `wos-workflow.schema.json` — not via a standalone top-level `$wosWorkflowGovernance` marker (ADR 0076).

Processors must enforce schema conditionals (e.g. `governance` required for rights-impacting and safety-impacting `impactLevel`) and, when loading `$wosDelivery` / `$wosOntologyAlignment` sidecars, ensure `targetWorkflow` binds to the intended workflow `url`.
Governance rules targeting non-existent tags or roles should produce warnings rather than hard-failing.

behaviorEssentials: AI Integration sidecars register agents as actors and impose deontic constraints (Permission, Prohibition, Obligation, Right) on their behavior.
Effective autonomy levels for agent actions are capped by the kernel impactLevel; rights-impacting workflows default to assistive.
The Formspec-as-validator pattern cages agent output as untrusted input validated against the same contract used by humans.
Narrative tier provenance generated by models MUST be explicitly labeled as non-authoritative.
conformanceEssentials: Agent registration, deontic constraints, and drift-related AI controls are expressed under `agents`, `aiOversight`, and related leaves inside a `$wosWorkflow` envelope validated by `wos-workflow.schema.json` — not via a standalone `$wosAIIntegration` document marker (ADR 0076).
Processor guardrail enforcement MUST execute in order: Permission -> Prohibition -> Obligation -> Confidence Floor.
Fallback chains MUST terminate in a human-action result or failure to ensure graceful degradation.

behaviorEssentials: Declare structured metadata for correspondence stored in case state without adding new event types.
Use entry templates to define channel, direction, and actorType requirements for categories of communication.
Populate base metadata (templateRef, contentRef, summary, timestamp) for every recorded correspondence entry.
Store pointers to external content (contentRef) rather than the content itself within the case record.
conformanceEssentials: Correspondence entry templates and channel metadata are authored under the `correspondence` block of a `$wosDelivery` sidecar (`$wosDelivery: "1.0"`, `targetWorkflow` joining the workflow URI) per `sidecars/wos-delivery.schema.json` — not via a standalone `$wosCorrespondenceMetadata` marker (ADR 0076).
Templates MUST use only allowed channel, direction, and actorType enum values or x-prefixed extensions.
Processors SHOULD allow multiple delivery sidecars targeting the same workflow to merge correspondence config additively where the schema permits.

behaviorEssentials: Externalize date-indexed business rules as temporal parameters resolved against a date in the case state.
Support value-over-time resolution (OpenFisca-style) for both scalar parameters and document bindings (URIs).
Resolve parameters at evaluation time using the most recent effective date relative to the resolution date cursor.
Inject resolved values into the execution context (under parameters.X) for use in FEL expressions and transitions.
conformanceEssentials: Temporal policy parameters are expressed under `governance.policyParameters` (and related governance leaves) inside a `$wosWorkflow` document validated by `wos-workflow.schema.json` — not via a standalone `$wosPolicyParameters` marker (ADR 0076).
Resolution MUST be deterministic: only one value per parameter is effective at any given point in time.
Processors should warn on parameter resolution gaps where the resolution date precedes the earliest effective date.

behaviorEssentials: Monitor agent behavioral stability over time using statistical metrics (PSI, KS) and performance benchmarks (accuracy).
Detect 'rubber-stamping' where human reviewers agree with agent output at rates or speeds that indicate a lack of engagement.
Define automated remediation actions (alerts, demotion to assistive, circuit breaking) triggered by drift threshold violations.
Manage model version transitions through shadow, canary, and production phases to ensure governance continuity.
conformanceEssentials: Drift monitoring and remediation configuration are expressed under `agents` / `aiOversight` (e.g. `driftMonitoring`, `driftDetection`) inside a `$wosWorkflow` envelope validated by `wos-workflow.schema.json` — not via a standalone `$wosDriftMonitor` marker (ADR 0076).
Statistical methods and evaluation windows MUST be applied as declared for each monitored agent.
Processors MUST verify that shadow-mode deployments do not alter the authoritative case state or provenance.

behaviorEssentials: Standardize semantic alignments between WOS case state and global vocabularies (JSON-LD, Schema.org).
Map WOS provenance records to PROV-O RDF shapes for cross-organizational audit exchange.
Establish SHACL shapes for formal data quality verification of WOS documents and instances.
Enable export to IEEE 1849 (XES) format for advanced process mining and bottleneck analysis.
conformanceEssentials: Linked-data and export semantics (JSON-LD @context, SHACL, PROV-O, process-mining config) are authored in a `$wosOntologyAlignment` sidecar validated by `sidecars/wos-ontology-alignment.schema.json` with `targetWorkflow` binding to the kernel — not via a standalone `$wosSemanticProfile` marker (ADR 0076 D-3).
JSON-LD context bindings MUST resolve to the declared ontology URIs.
Semantic exports MUST preserve the WOS impactLevel and auditLayer metadata as required for provenance integrity.

behaviorEssentials: Signature ceremonies bind signer roles, signing order, routed vs free-for-all flows, and evidence requirements to workflow transitions.
Intent capture, identity binding, and document binding align with Trellis attestation and export-bundle composition.
Decline, void, reassignment, reminders, and expiry are workflow-governed policy hooks with provenance on each state change.
SignatureAffirmation records join kernel Facts-tier provenance when a signing act is accepted.
conformanceEssentials: Author-time signing semantics for DocuSign-tier workflows live in the `signature` embedded block of a `$wosWorkflow` document validated by `wos-workflow.schema.json` (ADR 0076 D-2); this profile document remains canonical for SIG-* lint citations until inner-block descriptions fully absorb.
Processors MUST enforce signer coverage and transition gating declared in the embedded signature block when any transition uses kind `signature`.
Cryptographic certificate-of-completion bundles remain Trellis-owned; WOS governs workflow semantics and semantic evidence only.
